summary.intent: Address five Codex review findings on PR #724 for ars-ui field/form adapters in a single batched commit, covering reactive Leptos Field root state, ARIA submit prevention, controlled Dioxus input value reapplication, native disabled placement, and submit lifecycle completion.
summary.outcome: All five review items were fixed across ars-components, ars-leptos, and ars-dioxus with accompanying wasm browser tests; fmt, clippy, adapter parity, and SSR/native tests passed; changes committed as a single batched commit and pushed.

repo: The repo enforces a zero-warning rule — unused #[expect] annotations must be removed before committing.
Codex reviews are triggered by posting '@codex review' as a comment on the PR via gh api.
The agnostic Field API should only emit aria-disabled, not native disabled; native disabled belongs in adapter input helpers.
Dioxus controlled inputs require using the native value: prop rather than the generic AttrMap path so rejected/normalized edits can be reapplied to the DOM.
ARIA form mode must prevent native navigation even when no submit callback is provided.
The form machine lifecycle must be completed (SubmitComplete) after intercepted submissions, not left in a stuck-busy state.
Leptos Field root and native input attributes must be derived reactively from the live machine snapshot, not computed once at render.
Adapter parity is verified via cargo xtask ci adapter-parity.
Wasm browser tests require wasm-bindgen-test-runner to bind a local browser server and cannot run inside the sandbox.

code.finding: Core Field input attrs snapshot test was updated to assert aria-disabled only, removing native disabled from the agnostic API surface.
Root and native input attributes made reactive from live machine snapshot; previously computed statically at render time.
Controlled input value extracted from AttrMap and rendered via Dioxus native value: prop to enable DOM reapplication after rejected edits.
ARIA form now prevents native navigation by default even without a submit callback; intercepted submissions now complete the machine lifecycle.
Same submit semantics as Leptos Form applied for adapter parity.
Unnecessary #[expect] annotation removed from new controlled-value wasm test to satisfy zero-warning rule.

workflow: Batch all review fixes before pushing — do not push partial review fixes mid-session.
Accept insta snapshot .snap.new files by mv rather than re-running with UPDATE_EXPECT to keep the accept step explicit and auditable.
Run cargo fmt --all after clippy because formatting changes source text and a post-fmt clippy recheck is needed to confirm cleanliness.
Use focused test filters (--test field, --test form) for faster feedback before running full wasm suite.
Poll gh pr checks after a push rather than assuming checks attach immediately — new head SHA may take time to register workflow runs.
When apply_patch misses due to nearby context, split into smaller targeted edits rather than retrying the full patch.

friction: apply_patch missed on first attempt for Leptos Field root attributes due to nearby context mismatch, requiring the patch to be split into smaller edits.
cargo test does not accept multiple test name filters in a single invocation — initial commands were malformed and had to be split.
wasm-bindgen-test-runner cannot spawn its browser server inside the sandbox, requiring tests to be rerun outside it.
GitHub checks did not attach to the new head SHA immediately after push, requiring repeated polling before CI state was visible.
cargo fmt found a trailing comma style change after the clippy pass, requiring an extra fmt + re-clippy cycle.
open_items: The known upstream 'block' clippy notice is suppressed but not resolved — flagged as a pre-existing issue to revisit when upstream fixes land.
Wasm browser tests for Dioxus controlled-value reapplication cover the rejection case but the broader controlled input contract (e.g. async normalization) is not yet tested.

Native readonly handling in the agnostic Field API was explicitly left as-is (appropriate for native inputs); the review's disabled concern was the only item actioned — readonly scope may warrant a follow-up review thread.
